title: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean
description: This beloved Korean bakery treat offers a delightful symphony of flavors: soft, sweet bread generously filled with creamy, tangy cream cheese, then drenched in a rich, garlicky, and subtly sweet butter sauce. It's a modern Korean classic that perfectly balances savory and sweet notes with a luxurious texture, making it an irresistible snack or breakfast item.

ingredients.notes: Or full-fat milk, for the cream cheese filling
Optional, for the cream cheese filling
Melted, for the garlic butter sauce
Or condensed milk, for the garlic butter sauce
E.g., Ottogi brand for authentic flavor, or any good quality full-fat mayo
Finely chopped, plus extra for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Take each soft round milk bread bun and carefully slice it into 6 or 8 equal wedges, cutting almost all the way through to the base but leaving the bottom intact so the bun remains a single piece.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, heavy cream (or milk), vanilla extract (if using), and a pinch of salt. Using a whisk or spatula, mix until the filling is completely smooth and creamy with no lumps. Transfer this mixture into a piping bag fitted with a wide round tip, or use a spoon.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Sauce
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, finely minced garlic, honey (or condensed milk), Korean mayonnaise, the large egg yolk, and a pinch of salt until well combined and emulsified. Stir in 1 tablespoon of finely chopped fresh parsley. The sauce should be fragrant and slightly thick.

instructions.title: Fill the Bread Buns
instructions.text: Carefully open the sliced wedges of each bread bun. Pipe or spoon a generous amount of the cream cheese filling into each crevice, ensuring an even distribution. Be careful not to overfill, but make sure each wedge has a good portion.

instructions.title: Coat with Garlic Butter Sauce
instructions.text: Submerge each filled bun completely into the garlic butter sauce, ensuring all sides, top, and especially the crevices between the wedges are thoroughly coated. You can also use a pastry brush to ensure even and ample coverage. Place the coated buns onto the prepared baking sheet.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Bake the cream cheese garlic bread in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown on the outside and the cream cheese filling is warm, soft, and slightly gooey. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning the garlic.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the cream cheese garlic bread from the oven. Immediately garnish with an extra sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley. Serve warm to enjoy the full explosion of flavors and textures.

tips: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor; pre-minced jarred garlic lacks the same pungent freshness.
Do not overbake the bread; it should be golden brown and slightly crispy on the outside, but remain soft and moist inside.
This bread is best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven, when the cream cheese is gooey and the garlic butter is fragrant.
article:
## History of Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean

Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, or 'Maneulppang' (마늘빵) with 'Keurimchijeu' (크림치즈), is a relatively modern but immensely popular culinary innovation in South Korea. While garlic bread has Western origins, its cream cheese-filled version became a viral sensation in Korean bakeries and street food stalls, particularly in the late 2010s. It exemplifies the Korean penchant for blending global flavors with their unique sweet-savory palate and creamy textures. This dish quickly evolved from a simple bakery item into a must-try delicacy, reflecting Korea's dynamic food culture that embraces fusion and creates distinct culinary trends. It's a testament to how Korean cuisine continually reinvents and elevates international dishes to suit local tastes.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean works because it masterfully balances a symphony of flavors and textures that appeal directly to the Korean palate. The foundation is a soft, slightly sweet milk bread bun, which acts as a perfect sponge for the rich garlic butter sauce. The sauce itself is a revelation – a luscious blend of savory butter and garlic, enriched with the subtle sweetness of honey (or condensed milk) and the creamy depth of mayonnaise, all bound by an egg yolk. This creates an unctuous, flavorful coating. The star, however, is the cream cheese filling, which introduces a tangy, sweet, and cool counterpoint to the warm, savory exterior. This contrast cuts through the richness, preventing the dish from being cloying and making each bite incredibly addictive. The outer crust becomes slightly crisp during baking, while the interior remains soft and moist, offering a delightful textural play.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

1.  **Quality of Garlic**: Always use fresh garlic and mince it yourself. Pre-minced garlic from a jar often has a less vibrant flavor and can sometimes carry an off-note. The pungency and aroma of freshly minced garlic are crucial for the authentic taste.
2.  **Proper Bun Slicing**: Ensure you slice the buns deeply enough to create substantial pockets for the cream cheese filling, but do not cut all the way through. The base must remain intact to hold the bun together and allow it to soak up the garlic butter evenly.
3.  **Generous Coating**: Do not be shy with the garlic butter sauce. The key to a truly decadent Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is a thorough and generous coating. Each crevice and surface of the bun should be drenched to achieve that signature moist, intensely flavorful result.
4.  **Serve Immediately**: This bread is absolutely best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven. The cream cheese filling will be wonderfully gooey, and the garlic butter sauce will be at its most aromatic and absorbed into the soft bread, creating an unparalleled experience.

## Serving Suggestions

Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

1.  **Cafe Companion**: Pair it with a hot Americano or a refreshing iced coffee, a classic combination found in Korean cafes, making for a perfect afternoon pick-me-up.
2.  **Breakfast or Brunch Indulgence**: Serve it as a decadent breakfast or brunch item, perhaps alongside a simple fruit salad to balance the richness.
3.  **Sweet-Savory Snack**: Enjoy it on its own as a satisfying and flavorful snack any time of the day. Its unique profile makes it a fulfilling treat.
4.  **Unique Appetizer**: For gatherings, slice the baked bread into smaller, bite-sized pieces. It makes a distinctive and impressive appetizer that will surely be a conversation starter and a crowd-pleaser.
5.  **Dessert Alternative**: While rich, its sweetness makes it suitable as an unconventional dessert, especially for those who appreciate a less saccharine end to a meal.

jsonLd.name: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean
jsonLd.description: This beloved Korean bakery treat offers a delightful symphony of flavors: soft, sweet bread generously filled with creamy, tangy cream cheese, then drenched in a rich, garlicky, and subtly sweet butter sauce. It's a modern Korean classic that perfectly balances savory and sweet notes with a luxurious texture, making it an irresistible snack or breakfast item.
jsonLd.keywords: Korean, Korean, authentic, traditional, baking, Korean, baking, authentic, street food, garlic bread, cream cheese, sweet and savory, modern Korean
recipeIngredient: 4-6 Soft Round Milk Bread Buns (Unsliced, about 100-120g each)
200g (7 oz) Cream Cheese (Softened at room temperature)
30g (2 tbsp) Granulated Sugar (For the cream cheese filling)
1 tbsp Heavy Cream (Or full-fat milk, for the cream cheese filling)
1/2 tsp Vanilla Extract (Optional, for the cream cheese filling)
100g (7 tbsp) Unsalted Butter (Melted, for the garlic butter sauce)
30g (2 tbsp) Honey (Or condensed milk, for the garlic butter sauce)
30g (2 tbsp) Korean Mayonnaise (E.g., Ottogi brand for authentic flavor, or any good quality full-fat mayo)
1 large Egg Yolk (For the garlic butter sauce)
1 tbsp Fresh Parsley (Finely chopped, plus extra for garnish)
Pinch Salt (For both filling and sauce)

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Bread Buns
recipeInstructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Take each soft round milk bread bun and carefully slice it into 6 or 8 equal wedges, cutting almost all the way through to the base but leaving the bottom intact so the bun remains a single piece.

recipeInstructions.name: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
recipeInstructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, heavy cream (or milk), vanilla extract (if using), and a pinch of salt. Using a whisk or spatula, mix until the filling is completely smooth and creamy with no lumps. Transfer this mixture into a piping bag fitted with a wide round tip, or use a spoon.

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Garlic Butter Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, finely minced garlic, honey (or condensed milk), Korean mayonnaise, the large egg yolk, and a pinch of salt until well combined and emulsified. Stir in 1 tablespoon of finely chopped fresh parsley. The sauce should be fragrant and slightly thick.

recipeInstructions.name: Fill the Bread Buns
recipeInstructions.text: Carefully open the sliced wedges of each bread bun. Pipe or spoon a generous amount of the cream cheese filling into each crevice, ensuring an even distribution. Be careful not to overfill, but make sure each wedge has a good portion.

recipeInstructions.name: Coat with Garlic Butter Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Submerge each filled bun completely into the garlic butter sauce, ensuring all sides, top, and especially the crevices between the wedges are thoroughly coated. You can also use a pastry brush to ensure even and ample coverage. Place the coated buns onto the prepared baking sheet.

recipeInstructions.name: Bake to Golden Perfection
recipeInstructions.text: Bake the cream cheese garlic bread in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown on the outside and the cream cheese filling is warm, soft, and slightly gooey. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning the garlic.

recipeInstructions.name: Garnish and Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Once baked, remove the cream cheese garlic bread from the oven. Immediately garnish with an extra sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley. Serve warm to enjoy the full explosion of flavors and textures.
